lesson.menu_desc: Arbitration vocabulary and the first conditional in disputes -- 27 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 14 -- Resolving Disputes -- Arbitration & First Conditional
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Simone Quiles de Santana Marques | Aula 14 -- Resolving Disputes

listenings.text: Hi Simone, this is Daniel from the legal team. We have a problem with the German partner. They say we did not meet a deadline, and now there is a dispute about the last payment. I want your help before this gets worse. First, please read the dispute clause in the contract. It says that if the parties cannot agree, they will try mediation, and if mediation fails, they will go to arbitration in London. I do not want a court case, so mediation is a good first step. If we settle the claim early, we will save time and money. But if the partner refuses to talk, we will have to start arbitration, and the award will be binding on both sides. Please check whether we can appeal an arbitration award under this contract. If we cannot, we must be very careful. Let us talk on Thursday. If you have any questions before then, just call me. Thank you, and talk soon.

extra_audio.text: Daniel tells Simone that there is a dispute with the German partner about the last payment. Simone reads the dispute clause and sees that the parties will try mediation first. She suggests they settle the claim early to save time and money. The partner refuses to talk, so the parties start arbitration in London. The arbitrator gives a binding award, and both sides accept the decision.
